matches.rationale: The Bash rule is the combined pre-PR check for modified libraries and explicitly includes formatting in its named target. It also covers linting and testing, but the 1:1 constraint permits assigning this combined extraction to only one of the three gold rules.

The Edit rule blocks Sphinx-style double-backtick inline-code syntax, exactly matching the prohibited edit. Its file-path scope is narrower than the gold rule, which is still a match under the partial-coverage rule.

misses.note: Linting is covered only inside the single combined extracted rule already assigned to g1; 1:1 matching leaves no separate extracted rule for this gold rule.
Testing is covered only inside the single combined extracted rule already assigned to g1; 1:1 matching leaves no separate extracted rule for this gold rule.
Compiler-self-reported skip: "Note that the TEST env var can be used to run a specific test file or pass extra pytest options — describes an optional capability, not an action requirement."
The document's positive single-backtick instruction was not emitted as its own Edit rule; the only related extraction is the double-backtick prohibition already matched to g5.
